sources.citation: Mohr, P. J., Newell, D. B., Taylor, B. N., & Tiesinga, E. (2024). CODATA recommended values of the fundamental physical constants: 2022.
sources.notes: Canonical source for fundamental physical constants. Machine-readable table at https://physics.nist.gov/cuu/Constants/Table/allascii.txt.

sources.citation: Kramida, A., Ralchenko, Yu., Reader, J., and NIST ASD Team (2023). NIST Atomic Spectra Database, ver. 5.10.
sources.notes: Energy levels, lines, transition probabilities. Already in use via tools/ingest_nist.py.

sources.citation: Olver, F. W. J., Daalhuis, A. B. O., Lozier, D. W., Schneider, B. I., Boisvert, R. F., Clark, C. W., Miller, B. R., Saunders, B. V., Cohl, H. S., and McClain, M. A., eds. NIST Digital Library of Mathematical Functions.
sources.notes: Special functions (Gamma, Bessel, Legendre, Hermite, Laguerre, Gegenbauer, hypergeometric). Stable section URLs (e.g. dlmf.nist.gov/14.30) used for citation grain per A5.

sources.citation: CIE 015:2018, Colorimetry, 4th ed. Commission Internationale de l'Eclairage.
sources.notes: CIE 1931 2-deg standard observer CMFs. Used numerically; tabulated values widely available.

sources.citation: IEC 61966-2-1:1999, Multimedia systems and equipment - Colour measurement and management - Part 2-1: Colour management - Default RGB colour space - sRGB.
sources.notes: sRGB matrix and EOTF for XYZ -> linear sRGB -> gamma-encoded sRGB pipeline.

sources.citation: Ciddor, P. E. (1996). Refractive index of air: new equations for the visible and near infrared. Applied Optics, 35(9), 1566-1573.
sources.notes: Air<->vacuum default per cluster B5. Supersedes Edlen 1966 as IAU/NIST current standard.

sources.citation: Edlen, B. (1966). The Refractive Index of Air. Metrologia, 2(2), 71-80.
sources.notes: Didactic toggle alongside Ciddor 1996. Same retention pattern as Bruton 1996 vs CIE 1931.

sources.citation: Coxeter, H. S. M. (1973). Regular Polytopes (3rd ed.). Dover Publications. ISBN 978-0486614809.
sources.notes: Canonical reference for regular polytopes in all dimensions. Cite, do not paste.

sources.citation: Aroyo, M. I. (Ed.) (2016). International Tables for Crystallography Volume A: Space-group symmetry (6th ed.). Wiley. ISBN 978-0470974230.
sources.notes: 230 space groups, 32 crystallographic point groups, 14 Bravais lattices.

sources.citation: Morse, P. M., & Feshbach, H. (1953). Methods of Theoretical Physics, Vol. I. McGraw-Hill.
sources.notes: 11 separable coordinate systems in 3-D (ch. 5). Stackel theorem and separability.

sources.citation: Sansonetti, J. E., & Martin, W. C. (2005). Handbook of Basic Atomic Spectroscopic Data. Journal of Physical and Chemical Reference Data, 34(4), 1559-2259.
sources.notes: Ionization energies, persistent lines, ground configurations. NIST Handbook of basic atomic spectroscopic data.

sources.citation: Bransden, B. H., & Joachain, C. J. (2003). Physics of Atoms and Molecules (2nd ed.). Pearson Education. ISBN 978-0582356924.
sources.notes: Hydrogen wavefunction derivation, term-symbol theory, Zeeman/Stark perturbation.

sources.citation: Demtroder, W. (2018). Atoms, Molecules and Photons (3rd ed.). Springer. ISBN 978-3662555217.
sources.notes: Line-broadening models (natural, Doppler, pressure). Laser/coherence physics for v2 photon animation.

sources.citation: Bruton, D. (1996). Approximate RGB values for Visible Wavelengths. Stephen F. Austin State University.
sources.notes: Didactic color toggle. The familiar textbook colors of the Balmer plate. Never the default.

sources.citation: Wyman, C., Sloan, P-P., & Shirley, P. (2013). Simple Analytic Approximations to the CIE XYZ Color Matching Functions. Journal of Computer Graphics Techniques, 2(2), 1-11.
sources.notes: Analytic approximation to CIE 1931 CMFs as a fallback when the full table is not desired.
